# Win recommendations — Synthetic-Bid-001

## Win-factor coverage

| # | Factor | Score | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Lead with the boldest solution** | ✅ | Cloud-native architecture proposed with outcome-based SLAs; aligns with client's digital-first mandate. |
| 2 | **Hook narrative with opening stat** | ⚠️ | No opening shocking stat or provocative question found; deck opens with a company overview slide. |
| 3 | **Preemptively destroy the boutique objection** | ❌ | No Big 4 alumni credentials, insurance-backed guarantees, or objection-killing material referenced in the first five slides. |

---

## Top 3 moves to raise win probability

**1. Open with the cost-of-inaction number (Factor 2)**
The client's legacy ERP runs without SLA enforcement for 18 months. Quantify the monthly cost of an unmanaged P1 incident (estimated €120K per event). Table that number on slide 1 before any rate card appears. This is the single mechanism that removes price from the evaluation axis.

**2. Add a proof metric to the references slide (Factor 1)**
The existing reference (DemoRef SAP AMS) is live but cited without numbers. Pull at least one metric — ticket resolution time improvement, SLA compliance rate — and attach it to the pricing section. Without a number the MSP structure is a rate card; with a number it becomes a proof-backed outcome commitment.

**3. Destroy the boutique objection in the first five slides (Factor 3)**
Add a named Big 4 alumni profile or an insurance-backed delivery guarantee to the executive summary. Place it before the scope/approach section. Evaluators are briefed on boutique risk; this slide kills that objection before it can form.

---

## Estimated win-rate lever

Quantifying the cost of the 18-month ERP support gap at €120K/incident removes price from the evaluation axis and forces the client to choose on risk — where our delivery record is unmatched.
